rationale: The cited paper is used to support specific empirical claims about key moderators in cause-related marketing, especially the possibility of counterintuitive fit effects and donation-size-related variation. In the citing article, it is not just listed in the bibliography; it appears in the conceptual setup and moderator overview that motivate the meta-analysis. This means it meaningfully informs how the citing study frames heterogeneity and which moderator domains it synthesizes.
relation_description: The meta-analysis uses Koschate-Fischer et al. (2012) as prior empirical evidence for inconsistent moderator findings in cause-related marketing, particularly around fit and donation-related effects. The cited study helps motivate and structure the moderator framework that the citing paper quantitatively synthesizes.
